assessments.status_rationale: Today’s bullets focus on the Iran war and its economic/geopolitical effects; there is no state-media-confirmed announcement or indication of a formal succession decision recognizing Mojtaba Khamenei as Supreme Leader.
News mentions an oil spike to around $100 and then a sharp drop (CNBC notes oil plunging ~10%), but nothing indicates Brent front-month settling at or above $150 on any day.
No market-volatility datapoint is provided in the bullets (no VIX close values), so there’s no clear evidence that VIX has closed at or above 40.
Nothing in today’s items references TBAC materials or any explicit TBAC slide/section titled with 'stablecoin(s)'.
The Asia-related coverage concerns regional economic fallout from the Middle East conflict, but does not report a South Korea market-stabilization package with ≥₩20T equity-purchase capacity.
No bullet reports China announcing additional national-level fiscal stimulus of ≥CNY 1T beyond previously announced 2026 plans.
Today’s news does not indicate a U.S. federal funding lapse/shutdown lasting at least 24 consecutive hours.
No mention of USDOT/FHWA launching or awarding a multi-state satellite InSAR (or equivalent) pilot across at least five states.
None of the bullets report a major academic publisher announcing a single batch retraction of ≥200 papers tied to paper mills/fake peer review/fabrication.
